extraction_reasoning: The litigation table reports proceedings against the Company (criminal 2, tax 4, statutory/regulatory 5), against Directors (criminal 5, statutory/regulatory 2), and against Promoters (criminal 5, statutory/regulatory 2); I counted only these Against rows and excluded all By rows. Tax proceedings were kept separate from statutory/regulatory proceedings, and no subsidiary litigation row or Group Company proceedings were disclosed. The contingent-liabilities table is as of March 31, 2024 and lists Indian Railway ₹5.31 million, Income Tax ₹18.00 million and GST ₹2.75 million, but does not state a Total row; accordingly, the total contingent-liabilities field is null rather than re-summing the components. The company-wide customer concentration figures are for full Fiscal 2024 and are percentages of revenue from operations: top 5 customers 57.07% and top 10 customers 71.97%. Monetary litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million; the quantified litigation amount of ₹30.93 million converts to ₹3.093 crore (₹30.93 million ÷ 10).
